header.updateMccConfirm: Upgrade MCC to {version}? The service will restart and the page will briefly disconnect.

common.restartRequired: Saved. Restart required to take effect.

cron.noJobs: No cron jobs for this agent.

quickActions.title: Quick Actions
quickActions.confirmRestartOpenclaw: Restart OpenClaw? This will interrupt running tasks.
quickActions.confirmRestartTailscale: Restart Tailscale? Network may drop briefly.
quickActions.confirmKillTmux: Kill all tmux sessions? (including Claude persistent session)
quickActions.confirmSysUpdate: Run apt update + upgrade? This may take several minutes.
quickActions.confirmUpdateMcc: Upgrade Mission Control? The dashboard will restart automatically when the new version is in place.

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.subtitle: All agent sessions and their activity

cronJobs.title: Cron Job Manager
cronJobs.subtitle: OpenClaw Cron Jobs
cronJobs.confirmDeleteJob: Are you sure you want to delete this job? This action cannot be undone.
cronJobs.sectionBasicDesc: Name and description of the job
cronJobs.sectionExecutionDesc: Determine how to run and which model to use
cronJobs.sectionDeliveryDesc: How to notify after execution (Isolated mode only)
cronJobs.scheduleKindAtDesc: Run once at a specified time
cronJobs.fieldAtHint: ISO 8601 time or relative time (e.g., 20m = in 20 minutes, 2h = in 2 hours)
cronJobs.fieldEveryHint: Repeat at a fixed interval (e.g., 10m = every 10 minutes, 1h = every hour)
cronJobs.fieldTimezoneHint: Timezone for the cron expression (default Asia/Taipei)
cronJobs.fieldStaggerHint: Random delay window to prevent multiple jobs from starting simultaneously. Set 0 or leave empty for precise scheduling
cronJobs.sessionIsolatedDesc: Run in a dedicated session, doesn't affect main conversation
cronJobs.sessionMainDesc: Run as a system event in the main conversation's heartbeat
cronJobs.fieldMessagePlaceholder: Tell the agent what to do...
cronJobs.fieldMessageHint: Message the agent receives in Isolated mode
cronJobs.fieldSystemEventHint: System event injected into heartbeat in Main mode
cronJobs.fieldAgentHint: Optional, specify which agent to run. Leave empty for default agent
cronJobs.fieldModelHint: Optional, override the agent's default language model
cronJobs.fieldThinkingHint: Model thinking depth, only supported by some models (e.g., GPT-5.2, Codex)
cronJobs.fieldTimeoutHint: Abort if execution exceeds this many seconds
cronJobs.deliveryAnnounceDesc: Send results to a specified chat channel
cronJobs.deliveryWebhookDesc: POST results to a specified URL
cronJobs.fieldChannelHint: Delivery target channel (telegram / whatsapp / slack / discord / last)
cronJobs.fieldBestEffortHint: When checked, delivery failure won't cause the entire job to fail
cronJobs.fieldDeleteAfterRunHint: Enabled by default for one-time jobs, automatically deleted after successful execution
cronJobs.wakeNextHeartbeatDesc: Wait for the next scheduled heartbeat

browser.autoGenerateNote: Reports are auto-generated by OpenClaw at 08:00 daily

topicManager.formatTemplateDesc: This template defines the final output format. All topic results are merged using this template.
topicManager.firstTimeDescription: Load default topic templates and enable the morning report feature with one click. Includes AI Tech, Stocks, Crypto, Social Trends, ArXiv Papers, and Geopolitics.
topicManager.activateHint: Creates default topics, loads templates, enables scheduling
topicManager.generatePromptsDesc: Runs before the first topic, generating prompt files for all topics (with format template, previous day URL dedup, etc.).
topicManager.generatePromptsTemplateDesc: Agent message template for triggering prompt generation.
topicManager.podcastHarvestDesc: Runs one interval after the podcast trigger to collect the result from the dashboard and push the URL to the channel. The trigger agent never has to poll or time out.
topicManager.podcastHarvestTemplateDesc: Prompt for the harvest agent. GETs /api/morning-report?type=podcast-result&date=… and reports based on ready/status.
topicManager.simpleCurlJobHint: This agent only runs a curl + parses JSON + reports a URL — no reasoning required. A cheap small model (e.g. openai/gpt-5.4-mini, kimi/kimi-code) saves cost without affecting output.
topicManager.podcastPolishModelDesc: Used server-side to rewrite the morning-report markdown into a spoken podcast script. Pick a mid-tier or better model (e.g. openai/gpt-5.5-pro, kimi/kimi-code) — not mini, the output quality drop is audible.
topicManager.agentMessageTemplateDesc: Instructions sent to agent when cron triggers
topicManager.htmlTemplateDesc: HTML page template for merged report output
topicManager.polishTemplateDesc: AI Agent prompt to rewrite news summaries into conversational podcast scripts
topicManager.scriptTemplateDesc: Text template for podcast intro, transitions, and outro

topicCard.name: Name
topicCard.deliveryAnnounceDesc: Send results to a specified chat channel
topicCard.deliveryWebhookDesc: POST results to a specified URL

execution.oneClickRunDesc: Runs in order: Generate Prompts → Run Topics → Finalize → Generate Podcast

settings.title: Report Settings
settings.loadDefaultTemplatesDesc: Reset all topic prompt templates and the global format template to built-in defaults.
settings.loadDefaultTemplatesWarning: ⚠️ This will overwrite all your custom template modifications and cannot be undone.
settings.confirmOverwrite: Are you sure you want to overwrite all templates?

tunnel.title: Cloudflare Quick Tunnel
tunnel.description: Create a temporary public website for external access to morning report HTML. Uses Cloudflare Quick Tunnel, no account required.

settings.title: Settings
settings.backupDescription: Backup includes: OpenClaw config, cron jobs, local database

skills.title: Capture Skills
skills.intro: Install OpenClaw skills that write into your Obsidian vault.

install.pageSubtitle: Select components to install, then click Start Install
install.obsidianDesc: Virtual desktop environment to run Obsidian on server
install.nothingSelected: Please select at least one component

vaultDialog.title: Set Vault Path
vaultDialog.description: Obsidian is installed, but no existing vault was found. Please enter your Obsidian vault path so headless services can use it.
vaultDialog.label: Vault Path
vaultDialog.hint: Enter the path to your Obsidian vault folder. Use ~ for home directory.

services.memoryTooltip: Physical memory (RSS) currently used by this process

settings.title: Settings
settings.restartRequired: Some settings require a service restart to take effect
settings.e2eeEnabledDesc: Encrypt contents on the remote CouchDB database. Recommended when using LiveSync synchronization.
settings.e2eePassphraseWarn: ⚠ Please save this passphrase in a secure location. If lost, you will not be able to decrypt data in the remote database.
settings.e2eePathObfuscationDesc: Obfuscate file paths in the remote database. Requires rebuilding the database if changed.
settings.tailscaleEnableDesc: Lets devices signed into the same Tailscale account (phone, other computers) reach this CouchDB.
settings.tailscaleNotInstalled: Tailscale not detected. Install and sign into Tailscale first.
settings.tailscaleNotRunning: Tailscale is installed but not running or not signed in.
settings.httpsModeDesc: iOS Obsidian requires HTTPS to sync (Apple ATS restriction). Serve is recommended.
settings.httpsServeDesc: Uses a real Let's Encrypt certificate signed via Tailscale, accepted by iOS. Reach is still limited to your tailnet.
settings.httpsFunnelWarning: ⚠ Warning: Funnel exposes CouchDB to the entire public internet — anyone can reach it. Make sure CouchDB has a strong password and enable E2EE. Only use when necessary.
settings.funnelNotEnabledOnTailnet: Funnel is not yet enabled on your tailnet. Open the link below to enable it, then try again:
settings.httpsCertsNotEnabledOnTailnet: HTTPS certificates are not enabled for your tailnet, so the browser sees a self-signed cert. Enable it in the admin DNS settings, then try again:
settings.operatorSetupNeeded: Tailscale denied access: mission-control isn't authorised to operate tailscale. SSH into the server and run this once (it's remembered permanently):

uninstall.confirm: Are you sure? All services will be stopped and removed.
uninstall.confirmCouchdb: Are you sure? Database data will be permanently lost!
uninstall.deleteData: Also delete database files and settings
uninstall.deleteDataWarn: This will permanently delete all synced data and settings, but will not delete your vault

livesync.title: LiveSync Plugin
livesync.description: Automatically download and configure the Obsidian LiveSync plugin to connect with CouchDB for real-time sync

notebooklm.notInstalled: NotebookLM CLI is not installed. Please install it from the Setup page first.
notebooklm.notAuthDesc: Please log in with your Google account to use NotebookLM
notebooklm.browserRequired: Browser must be installed and running from the /browser page first
notebooklm.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this notebook? This cannot be undone.

sourcePanel.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this source? This cannot be undone.

chatPanel.title: Chat with Notebook

terminal.ptyNotAvailable: Terminal service is not available. Check server logs.
terminal.noSessions: No active sessions. Click + to start.

dashboard.scriptsNotFoundDesc: Configure the scripts directory in settings

dashboard.wizardStepSourceDesc: OpenClaw data is included by default. Optionally add extra folders.
dashboard.wizardStepScheduleDesc: Select an existing schedule or create a new one
dashboard.wizardStepJobDesc: Name your backup job and finish setup
dashboard.wizardDoneDesc: Your backup job is set up and will run automatically on schedule.

customerService.title: Customer Service
customerService.subtitle: Schedule control · customer memory · knowledge base

conversations.title: Conversations
conversations.empty: No conversations yet. They'll appear once a customer sends a LINE message.
conversations.noneSelected: Pick a customer on the left to start chatting
conversations.messagePlaceholder: Type a message to send as operator...
conversations.quickReplyPlaceholder: Add quick reply (press Enter to add, max 13)
conversations.fileWillBeSentAsLink: LINE has no native file message — sent as text with filename + link
conversations.aiQuickReplyEnable: AI suggest Quick replies (pre-baked answer options for the customer)
conversations.aiQuickReplyHelp: Quick reply is the row of tappable buttons LINE shows under a bot message — tap one and it sends back the button text as the customer's reply. When enabled, the AI watches your draft + the conversation history and suggests 3 button labels that match the context. Tap + to add to staging, or press ↻ to regenerate.

stats.recommendationsHint: Auto-derived nudges based on current conversations and storage state.

recommendations.awaitingReply: {count} customers waiting on a reply (oldest {ageMin}m). Consider taking over in the Conversations tab or checking why the agent isn't responding.
recommendations.pauseExpiring: {count} operator handoff(s) will auto-resume within 5 minutes. Re-toggle in the conversation view if you need more time.
recommendations.storageOver: Media storage at {currentMb} MB exceeds the warning threshold of {thresholdMb} MB. Shorten retention or raise the threshold in Settings → Storage.
recommendations.storageNear: Storage at {pct}% of the {thresholdMb} MB threshold. Adjust ahead of time if needed.
recommendations.missingProfiles: {count}/{total} customers have no LINE Profile — token may have expired or the customer removed the bot as a friend. Check Settings → LINE Channel.
recommendations.retentionNever: Retention is set to "Never delete" and storage has reached {gb} GB. Consider setting a sane window to prevent unbounded growth.

quickReplyLlm.title: Quick Reply LLM
quickReplyLlm.description: LLM used by the conversation composer's AI quick-reply suggestions. By default it reuses the mem0 config (cheap + fast). Uncheck the option below to point to a different model / endpoint.
quickReplyLlm.useMem0: Use the same LLM as mem0
quickReplyLlm.useMem0Hint: Reads the openclaw-mem0 MCP env from ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json (gemini-2.5-flash-lite by default)
quickReplyLlm.baseUrlHint: OpenAI-compatible endpoint — typically ends in /v1 or /v1beta/openai/
quickReplyLlm.apiKeyPlaceholder: Paste API key (not echoed in UI)
quickReplyLlm.apiKeyHint: Falls back to mem0's key when empty
quickReplyLlm.countHint: How many quick-reply buttons the AI generates per draft ({min}–{max}). LINE's hard limit is 13.

storage.title: Storage
storage.description: Retention policy for customer-sent images, files, and audio. Text messages are kept forever and are never swept.
storage.retentionHelp: Files older than this are deleted. The message row is kept as a placeholder reading "[file expired]".
storage.thresholdHelp: When usage exceeds this, the bell icon shows a red badge and a toast is dispatched.

lineChannel.title: LINE Channel credentials
lineChannel.description: Stores the Channel ID / Channel Secret / Channel Access Token needed for the LINE Messaging API. The Conversations feature (operator handoff, push messages, profile fetch) only works once these are saved.
lineChannel.help: Find all three values in LINE Developer Console → your Channel. Channel Secret is mainly used for webhook signature verification; the Conversations feature only needs the Channel Access Token. Update here after re-issuing a token.

wiki.title: Knowledge entries
wiki.description: Create, edit, and delete knowledge entries used by the customer-service agent. The OpenClaw wiki index recompiles automatically after each save.
wiki.empty: No entries yet. Click "Add entry" to create one.
wiki.contentHint: Markdown supported. The title is used to generate the filename and page id — keep it short and clear.
wiki.deleteConfirmBody: The file {name} will be removed from the wiki vault. This cannot be undone.

handoff.title: Human handoff destinations
handoff.description: When the AI decides a customer needs a human, it calls Telegram + Email tools in parallel (one failing doesn't block the other). Configure both destinations here. Saving restarts the Gateway so the new env vars take effect.

telegram.title: Telegram
telegram.description: Handoff messages are sent from this bot to this chat_id. Get chat_id from @userinfobot (positive = user, negative = group).
telegram.botTokenHint: BotFather token (123456:ABC-...). Already-set values are masked; leave alone if you don't need to change.
telegram.chatIdHint: The chat where the team receives handoff notifications.

email.title: AgentMail (Email)
email.description: Sent via the AgentMail SDK. Needs an API key, a sender inbox, and a destination address.
email.inboxIdHint: An AgentMail inbox you've created — used as the From address.
email.toHint: Team email address. Single recipient only — use a group alias if multiple.

messages.saveSuccess: Saved. Gateway restarted with new env vars applied.

notInstalled.title: openclaw-handoff MCP server is not registered
notInstalled.description: Cannot find mcp.servers.openclaw-handoff in ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json. Run the openclaw-line-cs-agent skill to install the customer-service agent, or register the server manually, then return to this page.

customerService.description: Keep the AI assistant quiet during human-staffed hours. After hours, the AI takes over so customers don't go unanswered.
customerService.howItWorks: Powered by the OpenClaw plugin business-hours-gate. It claims inbound messages during configured windows — silence by default, or send a fixed reply. Saving config restarts the Gateway automatically.

linePatch.title: LINE webhook patch
linePatch.description: OpenClaw awaits the full agent turn before returning 200 to LINE, but LINE's webhook timeout is 1 second — so it retries and customers get duplicates. The patch makes webhooks ack 200 immediately and run the agent in the background.

rows.distPatchedOk: All three webhook handlers async-ack (covers the third plugin-bound handler added in 4.29)
rows.distPatchedBad: OpenClaw dist not patched; LINE will time out and retry (customers see duplicates)
rows.dropinInstalledOk: Patch script runs before every gateway start; survives npm update -g openclaw
rows.dropinInstalledBad: Upgrading OpenClaw will overwrite the patch; install this drop-in to auto-reapply

actions.repair: Repair (apply patch + install drop-in + restart Gateway)

pluginInfo.name: Plugin name
pluginInfo.pathHint: Symlink-loaded: plugin runs from this source dir; not copied to ~/.openclaw/plugins.

master.activeDesc: Customer messages still pass through schedule logic — silenced only during business hours.
master.pausedDesc: Every inbound message is silenced regardless of schedule. Resume to restore normal behavior.
master.needsInstall: Install the plugin first to use this toggle.

schedule.title: Active windows
schedule.description: AI is silenced during these windows. Outside them, the AI replies normally.

reply.title: Canned reply
reply.description: What to send when a message arrives during an active window. Leave empty to stay silent.

channels.title: Apply to channels
channels.description: Leave all unchecked to gate every channel.

messages.installSuccess: Plugin installed and enabled; Gateway restarted
messages.pauseToggleApplied: AI reply state applied; Gateway restarted

modeSwitch.bridgeSuccess: Switched to bridge mode; Gateway restarted
modeSwitch.isolatedSuccess: Switched back to isolated; Gateway restarted

isolated-no-embedding.title: Currently isolated mode: plain text search
isolated-no-embedding.description: Your customer-service wiki is in isolated mode (data stays scoped to this agent) and uses plain text matching. To enable closer-to-semantic search — so customer wording that differs from wiki phrasing still hits — switch to bridge mode (shared wiki + lancedb embedding). Both are valid: weigh isolation vs. better recall. Choose in Second Brain.

embedding-not-bound.title: Embedding backend ready, wiki not wired
embedding-not-bound.description: memory-lancedb is running with embeddings, but memory-wiki search.backend is still local (text). To use semantic search on the wiki, switch wiki search to shared in Second Brain.

memory.title: Long-term customer memory (mem0)
memory.description: Self-hosted mem0 stack: Qdrant vector store + Ollama bge-m3 embeddings + cloud LLM for fact extraction. The AI assistant searches a customer's history at the start of each turn and adds new facts as it learns them.

backend.title: Customer memory storage mode
backend.description: The AI assistant stores per-customer memory in mem0 (Qdrant vector store). Clicking the button below rewrites the AGENTS.md memory section and restarts the gateway.
backend.switchHint: AGENTS.md memory section is managed by this page (between the cs:memory-mode markers). Other sections untouched.

injector.label: user_id injector
injector.explain: customer-id-injector plugin overrides user_id / wiki entity path on tool calls with the canonical LINE senderId. Without it the LLM passes the wrong user_id ~5% of the time.
injector.explainMissing: Install the business-hours-gate plugin from the Overview tab — it installs customer-id-injector at the same time. Without it the LLM gets the wrong user_id / wiki entity path ~5% of the time (memory lands on the wrong customer).

mem0.title: mem0 (Qdrant + LLM extraction)
mem0.desc: Current setup. Customer memories live in Qdrant vector store; the AI uses semantic search to recall.
bullets: Strong: automatic LLM fact extraction (no schema)
Weak: every add costs ~3-5s LLM extraction
Weak: human staff can't easily read (binary + extracted text)

wizard.title: mem0 stack install wizard
wizard.description: Detects Docker / Ollama / bge-m3 / Qdrant / uv / MCP registration. Installs what's missing, skips what's already up.
wizard.sudoNote: Needs sudo for apt install docker.io and curl-based ollama install. Other steps are user-scope.

provider.title: LLM provider
provider.description: Used for mem0 fact extraction and graph operations. Embedding stays on local Ollama (configured elsewhere).
provider.notRegistered: MCP server not registered yet. Run the install wizard first.

openai.name: OpenAI-compat
openai.desc: Any OpenAI-compatible endpoint: Gemini OAI-compat, OpenRouter, vLLM, LM Studio…

gemini.name: Gemini · native
gemini.desc: Google AI Studio API. Some features (cache, grounding) only on native SDK.

ollama.name: Ollama · offline
ollama.desc: Fully local, CPU only. Slower extraction (5-30s).

fields.apiKeyHint: Stored keys are masked in the response. Type a new value to overwrite.

browser.title: Memory browser
browser.description: Pick a customer from the list below (most recently active first) or paste a LINE userId at the bottom. Once selected you can view memories, search, and add.

fields.queryPlaceholder: e.g. what time does this customer prefer?
fields.manualUserIdPlaceholder: Or paste a user ID manually (not in the list)

tester.title: Manual tester
tester.description: Add a test memory for the selected customer (runs through fact extraction just like real AI interactions).
tester.placeholder: e.g. Customer usually meets on Thursday afternoons.

browser.noCustomers: No customers yet. They'll appear after their first LINE message reaches the bot.

errors.userIdRequired: Pick a customer or paste a user ID.
errors.userIdAndQueryRequired: User ID and query both required.
errors.userIdAndContentRequired: User ID and content both required.

stats.title: Usage stats
stats.description: Last 7 days of mem0 tool calls, latency, unique customers. Sourced from server.py telemetry — a local jsonl file.

noData.title: No telemetry data yet
noData.description: The AI assistant hasn't called mem0 tools yet, or server.py hasn't been started. Triggering an add/search from LINE will start producing data.

neo4j.title: Knowledge graph (graph mode)
neo4j.description: In theory mem0's graph mode would extract entities + relations on every add to support multi-hop questions.

unavailable.title: mem0 OSS 2.x does not ship graph_store
unavailable.description: We tested it directly: mem0 OSS 2.0.x does not include the graph_store module — graph is cloud-only (mem0 Platform). Installing Neo4j or kuzu locally won't help; the Memory object built from config silently ignores the graph_store key.

cloud.title: Upgrade to mem0 Cloud
cloud.description: Needs an API key; pay-per-use. Full graph + entity linking. But this contradicts your self-host requirement.

metadata.title: Simulate light relations with metadata
metadata.description: Tag entities into each memory's metadata (e.g. metadata.entities=["502","4-person room"]). Search can filter on these. We can add a metadata extraction hook for this.

future.title: Wait for mem0 OSS to ship graph
future.description: The mem0 team has discussed bringing graph back to OSS in GitHub issues. If you're not in a hurry, that's the simplest path.

settings.title: Global settings
settings.description: Page-wide preferences and the bound agent.

agents.title: Bound agent
agents.description: All settings on this page (schedule, memory) apply to this agent. Click an agent card to jump to /agents and view/edit its full configuration.
agents.empty: No agents found. Create one in /agents first.

detection.title: Detection rule (no hardcoded names)
detection.line-binding: Picked the agent with a LINE channel binding in openclaw.json. Highest priority — this is the agent actually handling LINE traffic.
detection.non-main: No LINE binding; falling back to the first non-main agent (main is usually the operator's own; CS should be separate).
detection.first: No specific match; using the first agent in the list. Tip: create a dedicated CS agent in /agents.
detection.none: No agents at all. Create one in /agents first.

search.title: Web Search Settings
search.description: Search is a vital feature of 🦞OpenClaw. Without web search, 🦞OpenClaw can't help you get things done.
search.tavilyInfo: Tavily.com currently offers 1,000 free searches per month, which is sufficient for typical usage.
search.step1Desc: Visit the Tavily website to sign up and obtain your API Key.
search.step2Desc: Paste the API Key you received in the field below.
search.step3Desc: Settings will be saved to the OpenClaw config and the Gateway will restart.
search.saveError: Failed to save, please try again later

notebooklm.title: NotebookLM CLI Setup
notebooklm.description: NotebookLM CLI enables 🦞OpenClaw to interact with Google NotebookLM for generating podcasts and other content.
notebooklm.uvInfo: The installer will automatically set up uv (Python package manager) and notebooklm-mcp-cli.

tailscale.description: Tailscale provides a zero-config VPN that securely connects your devices. Once set up, you can access this server from any device via its Tailscale IP.
tailscale.authInstructions: Click the link below or copy it to your browser to complete Tailscale login:
tailscale.authWaiting: Waiting for authentication... Status will update automatically once authorized.

imunifyav.description: Install ImunifyAV — a free Linux server antivirus that detects malicious files including web shells, viruses, hacker tools, and more. Once installed, it auto-updates via the system package manager (apt/yum).
imunifyav.uninstallDesc: Remove the program but keep config files and scan data for future reinstallation
imunifyav.uninstallSuccess: ImunifyAV has been uninstalled (config and data preserved)
imunifyav.purgeDesc: Remove the program and delete all config files, data, and state
imunifyav.purgeSuccess: ImunifyAV has been fully removed (including config and data)
